cold_start: ~200 ms typical (90% of allocations under 200 ms; up to 300 sandboxes/sec/cluster; pre-warmed pods enable sub-second creation)
isolation: gVisor (also supports Kata Containers and other OCI-compatible runtimes; runtimeClassName: gvisor)
name: GKE Agent Sandbox
notes: Pod Snapshots (memory-preserving suspend/resume) is a GKE-exclusive preview feature; the open-source CRD is alpha (extensions.agents.x-k8s.io/v1alpha1). The default security profile forbids privileged mode, hostPath, hostPID/hostIPC, and capabilities.add, so Docker-in-Docker is not supported in default sandboxes.
price_headline: $0.0445/vCPU-hour and $0.0049225/GiB-hour (GKE Autopilot, US regions; Agent Sandbox adds no charge)
pricing_model: No additional charge for Agent Sandbox; standard GKE resource pricing (per-second, vCPU/memory/storage) applies
summary: GKE Agent Sandbox is a managed GKE add-on, built on the open-source kubernetes-sigs/agent-sandbox project, that runs untrusted AI-generated code in isolated, stateful, single-replica Pods with persistent storage and kernel-level isolation via gVisor. It is used by AI agent runtimes, code interpreters, and development environments that need to execute code safely.
